claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 18 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start: **How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my three children. 

There's Alex, who is 3 years old, Blake, who is 8, and Charlie, who is 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's lovely that you have three children – Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (10).

Now I have a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

**Do all three children live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, not all of them. Alex and Charlie live with me, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Blake doesn't live with you, I need to understand a bit more about that situation.

**Could you tell me more about Blake's living arrangements? For example, does Blake live with another family member, are they in local authority care, or is there another arrangement?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake lives with their other parent. They've been in hospital for a while now, actually – a few months – but I still regularly buy things for them, like clothes and treats when I visit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for explaining that. I'm sorry to hear Blake has been in hospital for a while – I hope they're doing okay.

Just to make sure I understand correctly: **Blake normally lives with their other parent (not with you), and Blake is currently in hospital. Is that right?**

Also, **does Blake's other parent currently claim Child Benefit for Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Yes, that's right – Blake normally lives with their other parent and is currently in hospital.

I'm not sure if their other parent claims Child Benefit for Blake or not, to be honest.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Let me just gather a bit more information to complete the assessment.

**Are you responsible for Alex and Charlie in terms of their day-to-day care – such as providing food, clothing, and looking after them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex and Charlie live with me and I look after them day-to-day – I provide everything they need like food, clothing, and all their care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming.

Now I just need to check a couple more things:

**Are you living in the UK, and do you have the right to live in the UK (for example, are you a British or Irish citizen, or do you have settled or pre-settled status)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK – I'm in Manchester. And yes, I have the right to live here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just one more question to complete the picture:

**Are Alex (3) and Charlie (10) in full-time education or training? And specifically for Charlie, since they're 10, I just want to confirm they're under 16 – which they are – but are any of them receiving certain benefits in their own right, or are any of them married or in a civil partnership?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of that applies. Alex is only 3, so just at nursery age. Charlie is in primary school. Neither of them receives any benefits in their own right, and no, they're definitely not married or in a civil partnership – they're just kids!

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Let me read the relevant guidance to make sure I give you an accurate assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. I now have all the information I need to give you an assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility:

---

## Summary

**You appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, but not for Blake.**

---

### ✅ Alex (age 3) – **Eligible**
Alex lives with you and you're responsible for their day-to-day care. You live in the UK with the right to reside, and Alex meets all the requirements (under 16, not married, not receiving benefits in their own right).

---

### ❌ Blake (age 8) – **Not Eligible**
Although you buy things for Blake like clothes and treats when you visit, Blake normally lives with their other parent – not with you. Under the rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and the person the child lives with has priority. Blake's other parent would be the one entitled to claim Child Benefit for Blake. Even though Blake is currently in hospital, this doesn't change whose primary responsibility they are.

---

### ✅ Charlie (age 10) – **Eligible**
Charlie lives with you and you're responsible for their day-to-day care. You live in the UK with the right to reside, and Charlie meets all the requirements (under 16, not married, not receiving benefits in their own right).

---

### Next Steps
You can proceed to make a Child Benefit claim for **Alex and Charlie**. If you have any questions about Blake's situation or want to discuss who should be claiming for Blake, you could contact the Child Benefit Office for guidance.

I hope Blake makes a good recovery. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old, lives with you, and you are responsible for their day-to-day care (providing food, clothing, etc.). You live in the UK and have the right to reside here. Alex is under 16, not married/in a civil partnership, and does not receive benefits in their own right. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

Blake does not normally live with you – they live with their other parent. While Blake is currently in hospital and you contribute towards their upkeep by buying clothes and treats, the rules state that you can only claim Child Benefit for a child who lives with someone else if you are contributing at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their upkeep. However, the key issue here is that Blake's primary residence is with their other parent, not with you. The other parent would normally be the one eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake, as Blake lives with them. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and priority goes to the person the child lives with. Since Blake lives with their other parent and you're not sure if they claim Child Benefit, you would not be eligible to claim for Blake – the other parent would have the primary entitlement as the person Blake lives with.

Charlie is 10 years old, lives with you, and you are responsible for their day-to-day care (providing food, clothing, etc.). You live in the UK and have the right to reside here. Charlie is under 16, not married/in a civil partnership, and does not receive benefits in their own right. All eligibility criteria are met for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for two of your three children (Alex and Charlie), as they both live with you and you are responsible for their care. You meet the residency requirements as you live in the UK and have the right to reside here. However, you are not eligible to claim for Blake, as Blake normally lives with their other parent. The other parent would have priority for claiming Child Benefit for Blake since Blake lives with them. Only one person can receive Child Benefit for each child.

Charlie is under 16, not married/in a civil partnership, and does not receive benefits in their own right. All eligibility criteria are met for Charlie.

You meet the residency requirements as you live in the UK and have the right to reside here. However, you are not eligible to claim for Blake, as Blake normally lives with their other parent. The other parent would have priority for claiming Child Benefit for Blake since Blake lives with them. Only one person can receive Child Benefit for each child.
